rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. GREGORY YOCH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Martin Eng'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate The Prefiling Order And Remove Martin Eng From Vexatious Litigant List Per Ccp Sec 391.8
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 19, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG'S Motion To Vacate The Prefiling Order And Remove Martin Eng From Vexatious Litigant List Per Ccp Sec 391.8 Off calendar. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 391.8, an application to vacate a vexatious litigant prefiling order "shall be made before the justice or judge who entered the order, if that justice or judge is available," and if not, "before the presiding justice or presiding judge, or his or her designee." Here, Judge Charlotte Walter Woolard, who entered the prefiling order on February 2, 2011, has since retired from the bench, and accordingly is not available to hear the application. Accordingly, the application must be made to Presiding Judge Wong in Dept. 206. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. TURO INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And The Peoples Motion To Compel Further Responses To The Peoples First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents And Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 19, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To The Peoples First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents And Special Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. Rog 48. Turo should be prepared to address the People's argument that it is disingenuous for it to allege that overall lobbying spend by Enterprise or other traditional rental operators is relevant but that its (Turo's) overall lobbying spend is not relevant. RFP 3, 4. The parties should be prepared to discuss whether Turo's offer in footnote 2 of the Opposition resolves all issues re these RFPs; if not, to address what remains outstanding. The parties shall be prepared to discuss what search and production process will be required for those materials to be produced so the Court can determine an appropriate production deadline. RFP 33. The People shall be prepared to address whether the request to produce further responses is timely considering Turo's argument re the Oct. 2018 meet-and-confer letter (Lobo Decl. Ex. 10). CCP 2031.310(c); Sexton v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 1403 (45-day limit is "quasi-jurisdictional" because it implicates court power to rule on motion to compel). Turo shall be prepared to address the People's offer in the Reply pg. 5 to limit the City Offices/Departments. RFP 33, 37. On "reflecting," the People's requests clearly seek internal and other communications that "reflect" communications with San Francisco or marketing at SFO, not just communications directly "with" San Francisco or that "reference" SFO. Such internal communications ordinarily would be discoverable absent recognized privilege. The parties shall be prepared to address burden, including potential search methods and review techniques, so the Court can evaluate Turo's burden objection and any mechanisms for balancing those burdens with the right to discovery. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. VS. MICHAEL LIPSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 19, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT MICHAEL LIPSON DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Michael Lipson's demurrer to the first amended complaint is sustained with leave to amend. (See Code Civ. Proc., § 430.10(e).) Plaintiff has leave to amend to clearly plead the terms of the contract. The amended complaint presently refers to a 1990 written agreement but attaches an agreement that states that it will become effective in 2000. Plaintiff has leave to clarify the terms of the contract, e.g., whether the attached document superseded the parties' original agreement. The Court rejects Defendant's argument that Plaintiff must plead the terms of its merger agreement with predecessor-in-interest FIA Card Services, N.A. in order to establish standing to sue; the allegations of the complaint on that issue are sufficient. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: KELLAN DUNN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANICSCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance With A Court Order, Production Of Documents, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 19, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANTS FINE ARTS MUSEUMS OF SAN FRANCISCO, CORPORATION OF FINE ARTS MUSEUMS' Motion To Compel Compliance With A Court Order, Production Of Documents, And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. The parties shall be prepared to discuss whether there are any persons who would be identified in response to Form Interrogatory 215.1 other than those persons identified in the privilege log and Ms. Herrington's declaration. If there are, the parties should be prepared to address why the court should not require a full response to Form Interrogatory 215.1. To assist the parties prepare for hearing, my earlier order anticipated that Plaintiff would do something along the lines of identifying individuals interviewed so that any subsequent privilege log about withheld witness statements could be clear about who was a potential client and who was not. As to the RFPs, the parties should be prepared to address whether there are any outstanding documents to be produced. If all responsive documents have been produced in some manner, even if not the standard manner, I am not inclined to award sanctions other than costs of the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KELLAN DUNN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANICSCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Sanctions [Discovery]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 19, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Motion For Sanctions [Discovery] Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. The parties shall be prepared to discuss whether there are any responsive materials that Plaintiff has in his possession, custody, or control but has not yet produced. If there are such materials, the parties should be prepared to address why the court should not impose evidentiary sanctions prohibiting Plaintiff from introducing in subsequent proceedings any materials not already produced, and prohibiting the introduction of testimony about medical treatment or conditions that relies on materials not produced. CCP 2023.030(c). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KHEHRA DENTAL CORPORATION, ET AL VS. DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA,
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Stay
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 19, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA Motion To Stay Defendant Delta Dental's motion to stay is denied. The Court does not disagree that the Membership Action Committee's decision is reviewable under the common law doctrine of fair procedure and the MAPP Rules by way of administrative mandamus in Superior Court. (See, e.g., Delta Dental Plan of California v. Banasky (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 1598, 1605-1608.) However, Defendant has not conclusively established that such a claim is the only cause of action that Dr. Khehra may bring following the termination of her membership or contract, or that all of her causes of action solely constitute challenges to such termination. (See, e.g., Economy v. Sutter East Bay Hospitals (2019) 31 Cal.App.5th 1147, 1155 & fn. 6 [physician whose hospital privileges were suspended brought causes of action against hospital for violations of statutory and common law right to fair procedure, aiding and abetting, conspiracy, intentional interference with prospective economic advantage, and interference with right to practice profession, some of which were resolved against him on summary judgment prior to trial].) Further, Plaintiffs have not filed a petition for writ of mandate seeking such review and have disclaimed any intention to do so, and accordingly there is no pending mandate proceeding to which the Court should defer. Under the circumstances, the Court does not find that a stay of the action is warranted or is the appropriate procedural vehicle by which to address Defendant's defenses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ADA JACKSON VS. SWISSPORT, SA, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 19, 2019, Line 11, DEFENDANTS SWISSPORT SA, LLC, SWISSPORT USA, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration Defendants' Swissport SA, LLC and Swissport USA, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration is denied. The complaint in this action solely asserts representative claims under the California Private Attorneys General Act of 2004 (PAGA) for civil penalties for Defendants' alleged violations of the California Labor Code governing, among other things, provision of meal and rest breaks, payment of wages, provision of itemized wage statements, and indemnification of expenditures. Claims under PAGA are not arbitrable. (Iskanian v. CLS Transportation Los Angeles, LLC (2014) 59 Cal.4th 348, 384 ["where, as here, an employment agreement compels the waiver of representative claims under PAGA, it is contrary to public policy and unenforceable as a matter of state law."]; see also, e.g., ZA, N.A. v. Superior Court (2019) 8 Cal.5th 175, 197 ["Iskanian established an important principle: employers cannot compel employees to waive their right to enforce the state's interests when the PAGA has empowered employees to do so."].) This court previously stayed this action pending the California Supreme Court's decision in Z.B., N.A. In that case, the Court held that employees cannot recover unpaid wages described in Labor Code section 558 in a PAGA claim. (8 Cal.5th at 198 ["a PAGA claim does not include unpaid wages under section 558."].) Accordingly, the Court affirmed the Court of Appeal's judgment ordering the trial court to deny the employer's motion to compel arbitration. (Id.) Here, Plaintiff represents that in view of that holding, she "no longer intends to seek unpaid wages pursuant to Labor Code section 558," and that she is willing to stipulate to amend the complaint to strike any language which purports to seek such recovery. Accordingly, the motion to compel arbitration must be denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: HAIQING QIU VS. SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating, Evidence And Monetary Sanctions; Motion To Compel Responses And Deem Requests For Admissions, Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 19, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT DIGNITY COMMUNITY CARE DBA SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL'S Motion For Terminating, Evidence And Monetary Sanctions; Motion To Compel Responses And Deem Requests For Admissions, Admitted Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion for terminating sanctions is GRANTED. No timely opposition filed. "[C]ourts have long recognized that the terminating sanction is a drastic penalty and should be used sparingly." J.W. v. Watchtower Bible and Tract Socy. of New York, Inc. (2018) 29 Cal. App. 5th 1142, 1169. "The trial court should select a sanction that is 'tailor[ed] ... to the harm caused by the withheld discovery'" and does "not exceed that which is required to protect the interests of the party entitled to but denied discovery." Ibid. (internal citations omitted). Here, Plaintiff failed to comply with a clear order to appear for deposition. (Oct. 10, 2019, Order of Judge Pro Tem Gallo). CCP 2023.010(g). Plaintiff failed to respond to discovery requests seeking, among other things, evidence on which Plaintiff's successful opposition to the demurrer was based. CCP 2023.010(d). Additionally, Plaintiff failed to respond to requests for admissions, and Defendant's motion to deem admitted must be granted. CCP 2033.280. Deeming the requests for admissions admitted would for all practical purposes preclude introduction of evidence to support Plaintiff's claims. Rather than requiring a two-step process by which Defendant moves for summary judgment on the deemed admitted RFAs, I find that an order dismissing the action of Plaintiff against Dignity Community Care dba St. Francis Memorial Hospital is appropriate under CCP 2023.030(d). The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $4,015.80. CCP 2033.280(c) (sanctions are mandatory). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FARID SHARIF VS. HOWARD & 6TH STREET FOOD MARKET INC. A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 19, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT HOWARD & 6TH STREET FOOD MARKET INC.'S Motion For Reconsideration Defendant Howard & 6th Street Food Market, Inc.'s motion for reconsideration of the Court's October 29, 2019 order denying its motion to set aside default is denied. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Defendant Abdulrahman Khaled, the CEO of the moving corporate Defendant, denies that it was served with the summons and complaint in March 2019, and claims that he was not served as its agent for service of process until mid-August 2019. (Khaled Decl. 3, 4, 9.) However, Defendant did not make that claim when it moved to set aside the default, nor did it move to quash service of process. To the contrary, in its original motion, Defendant expressly admitted, "When Defendant was served, Defendant's CEO . . . talked with the other co-defendant" about defending the action. (Mot. at 2:11 (emphasis added).) It is too late for Defendant to change its story to claim that it was not served when it previously admitted that it was. Notably, Defendant did not even contest the Court's tentative ruling denying its motion to set aside the default or appear at the hearing. Further, Defendant has failed to show that it could not with reasonable diligence have presented at the original hearing the "new or different facts, circumstances or law" upon which it relies (even if they are true). Mr. Khaled admits that the facts he now presents in his new declaration "may have been known to [him] the entire time at the time the Motion was filed," but claims that he "provided my attorneys only an abridged version of what it discussed here." (Khaled Decl. 13.) That admission is fatal to his motion. (See Garcia v. Hejmadi (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 674, 690 ["The information consisting of Garcia's own declared knowledge was obviously always within his possession, and no satisfactory explanation appeared for not bringing it out earlier. The matters it addressed had been placed in issue by the motion, and there is no showing Garcia had been unavailable to counsel anytime during preparation of the initial opposition."]; see also Hennigan v. White (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 395, 406 [motion for reconsideration properly denied where, "[a]t the time of the [original] motion, Hennigan obviously was aware of the information contained in the new declaration"]; Morris v. AGFA Corp. (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 1452, 1460 [trial court properly denied motion for reconsideration based on evidence which should have been included in the initial opposition to the motion but was not, and thus was not a proper basis for reconsideration].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. As previously ordered, counsel for the plaintiff must also prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's October 29, 2019 tentative ruling. =(302/EPS)
